ddls:
CREATE TABLE "forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "t_start" INTEGER /* Start date of the forecast period */,
  "t_end" INTEGER /* End date of the forecast period */,
  "mean_r" REAL /* Mean value of the forecast */,
  "std_r" REAL /* Standard deviation of the forecast */,
  "quantile_0_025_r" REAL /* 0.025 quantile of the forecast */,
  "quantile_0_05_r" REAL /* 0.05 quantile of the forecast */,
  "quantile_0_25_r" REAL /* 0.25 quantile of the forecast */,
  "median_r" REAL /* Median value of the forecast */,
  "quantile_0_75_r" REAL /* 0.75 quantile of the forecast */,
  "quantile_0_95_r" REAL /* 0.95 quantile of the forecast */,
  "quantile_0_975_r" REAL /* 0.975 quantile of the forecast */,
  "data_date" TEXT /* Date of the data used for the forecast */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the age group */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the forecast */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the machine learning model used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecast_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_age_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_group_id") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the region */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the region */,
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the province */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the age group */,
  "min_age" INTEGER /* Minimum age of the age group */,
  "max_age" INTEGER /* Maximum age of the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the age group */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the demographic group */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the demographic group */,
  "income_level" TEXT /* Income level of the demographic group */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the demographic group */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the demographic entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the model */,
  "model_parameters" TEXT /* Parameters of the model */,
  "evaluation_metrics" TEXT /* Evaluation metrics of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_model_runs" (
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model run */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the model used */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the forecast produced */,
  "run_date" TEXT /* Date the model was run */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Parameters used for the model run */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the model run */,
  "started_by" INTEGER /* User who initiated the model run */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("run_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_model_runs_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecast_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_model_runs_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES forecasts ("forecast_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "data_date" TEXT /* Date of the data point */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data point */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who uploaded the data */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecast being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the forecast */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the forecast was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access */,
  "accessed_by" INTEGER /* User who accessed the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES forecasts ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the forecast used to make the prediction */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date the prediction was made */,
  "predicted_value" REAL /* Value of the prediction */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who made the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES forecasts ("forecast_id")
)
